summary: Stellar early‑'71 set with soaring Comes A Time and epic Dark Star jam
blurb: The show delivers tight phrasing on new material, highlighted by a soaring Comes A Time and powerful Jack Straw. Dark Star → Me & My Uncle flows into a rare Sitting On Top Of The World jam, while the first‑set classics stay crisp and energetic.
review: December 5, 1971 at New York’s Felt Forum captures the Grateful Dead in a transitional yet razor‑sharp form. Jerry Garcia is on fire – his phrasing and tone are described as “incredible” throughout, with a soaring vocal on Comes A Time that many reviewers called perfect. Keith Godchaux’s keyboards add depth alongside Pigpen, especially on Sugar Magnolia‑type numbers, while Phil Lesh’s bass work provides the storytelling backbone heard in Bertha. The first set rolls through fresh renditions of Jack Straw, Brown Eyed Women, El Paso and a tight “I Washed My Hands In Muddy Water,” keeping the momentum high. The second set is a showcase: a blistering Dark Star → Me & My Uncle sequence that blossoms into a rare Sitting On Top Of The World jam, plus Truckin’, Sugaree and a heartfelt Bertha. Across all recordings the band is consistently praised for being “locked in” and delivering one of the era’s most definitive performances.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the band's phrasing, vocal delivery, and historic jams (Dark Star, Comes A Time, Jack Straw). The consistency of high‑energy descriptors across sources justifies a strong +1.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp, clear mix that matches the original NYC radio broadcast; captures all standout songs in high fidelity without drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Tight early‑'71 set with fresh takes on new material and flawless phrasing.
Epic Dark Star → Me & My Uncle jam that evolves into a rare Sitting On Top Of The World excursion.
Soaring vocal performance on Comes A Time and powerful storytelling in Bertha.
Brilliant renditions of Jack Straw, Brown Eyed Women, El Paso and the iconic Truckin' jam.
song_highlights: I Washed My Hands In Muddy Water

band_performance.Jerry: Incredible phrasing and tone throughout; soaring vocals on Comes A Time and perfect guitar tone earn universal praise.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with lyrical storytelling highlighted in Bertha, adding depth to the set’s narrative flow.
band_performance.Bob: Strong rhythm work and vocal contributions (e.g., One More Saturday Night) noted as solid and smoky where appropriate.

must_listen_sequences: Sitting On Top Of The World
I Washed My Hands In Muddy Water
